note: Simpler witness than qiskit-commutative-cancellation-001: three gates on a single qubit, no second qubit involved. This one disproves a claim in our own report (Qiskit#16594), which said the fault needs a gate on another qubit between the two sxdg. It does not. sxdg sxdg sx combines to -pi/2, so one sxdg should survive; the pass emits nothing. Exhaustive sweep of all 62 sx/sxdg sequences up to length 5 on one qubit: 22 break, none at length 1 or 2. The breaking ones mix sx and sxdg and sum to a negative angle; a pure run survives, so sxdg sxdg sxdg is correct at -3pi/2 while sx sxdg sxdg sxdg sxdg drops everything at the same -3pi/2.
